findings.description: Document header alignment and styling differences.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the state seal logo and 'North Carolina Department of Health and Human Services' text are centered at the top of the page. In the HTML, they are left-aligned, and a horizontal dividing line has been added below the department name.

findings.description: Table header merged cell split and structure flattened.
findings.evidence: In the PDF table, the 'APPLICANTS' header spans 10 columns across a sub-row of numbered cells (1-10). In the HTML, this has been flattened into a single row of individual headers ('APPLICANTS 1', 'APPLICANTS 2', etc.), splitting the original merged cell.

findings.description: An errant hyperlink was applied to regular body text.
findings.evidence: In the HTML, the text 'helpful information. Pleas' is wrapped in a mailto link: 'helpful information. Please contact Mr. Johnson...'. In the PDF, this is plain text and only the actual email address is intended to be a link.
